COVID-19 Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)¶

Objective¶

The objective of this project is to perform Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) on a global COVID-19 dataset to understand the spread of the pandemic, identify the most affected countries and regions, analyze recovery and death trends, and generate actionable insights using Python.

1. Importing Required Libraries¶

In this section, we import the Python libraries required for data analysis and visualization.

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

2. Loading the Dataset¶

The dataset is loaded into a Pandas DataFrame for analysis.

In [2]:
df= pd.read_csv(r"C:\Users\ritik\OneDrive\Documents\NISHA\covid_19_clean_complete.csv")
df.head()
Out[2]:
Province/State Country/Region Lat Long Date Confirmed Deaths Recovered Active WHO Region

4 NaN Angola -11.20270 17.873900 2020-01-22 0 0 0 0 Africa

3. Understanding the Dataset¶

Before analysis, it is important to understand the structure of the dataset, including the number of rows, columns, data types, and summary statistics.

In [3]:
df.shape
Out[3]:
(49068, 10)

4. Data Quality Check¶

This step checks for missing values and duplicate records that may affect the accuracy of the analysis.

In [8]:
df.isnull().sum()
Out[8]:
Province/State    34404
Country/Region        0
Lat                   0
Long                  0
Date                  0
Confirmed             0
Deaths                0
Recovered             0
Active                0
WHO Region            0
dtype: int64
In [11]:
df.duplicated().sum()
Out[11]:
np.int64(0)

5. Data Preparation¶

The Date column is converted into datetime format to enable time-series analysis.

In [12]:
df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Date'])

Most Affected Countries¶

This analysis identifies the countries with the highest number of confirmed COVID-19 cases.

In [13]:
country_cases = df.groupby('Country/Region')[['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered','Active']].max()
country_cases.sort_values('Confirmed',ascending=False).head(10)
Out[13]:
Confirmed Deaths Recovered Active
Country/Region
US 4290259 148011 1325804 2816444
Brazil 2442375 87618 1846641 583080
India 1480073 33408 951166 495499
Russia 816680 13334 602249 245382
South Africa 452529 7067 274925 173590
Mexico 395489 44022 303810 53572
Peru 389717 18418 272547 108616
Chile 347923 9187 319954 82961
United Kingdom 300111 45759 533 254352
Iran 293606 15912 255144 34887

Countries with Highest Deaths¶

This analysis identifies the countries that reported the highest number of COVID-19 deaths. Understanding death counts helps evaluate the severity of the pandemic and the effectiveness of healthcare systems.

In [15]:
top10 = country_cases.sort_values('Confirmed',ascending=False).head(10)
top10['Confirmed'].plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('Top 10 Countries by Confirmed Cases')
plt.show()

Observation¶

The United States recorded the highest number of deaths, followed by Brazil, the United Kingdom, Mexico, and Italy.

Insight¶

Countries with large numbers of confirmed cases generally experienced higher death counts.

Business Impact¶

Monitoring death counts helps governments evaluate healthcare capacity and prioritize medical resources.

Countries with Highest Deaths¶

This analysis highlights the countries that reported the highest number of deaths.

In [16]:
country_cases.sort_values('Deaths',ascending=False).head(10)['Deaths'].plot(kind='bar')
plt.show()

Countries with Highest Recoveries¶

This analysis identifies countries with the highest number of recovered COVID-19 patients.

In [17]:
country_cases.sort_values('Recovered',ascending=False).head(10)['Recovered'].plot(kind='bar')
plt.show()

Observation¶

Brazil, the United States, and India reported the highest numbers of recovered patients.

Insight¶

Countries with large outbreaks also reported significant recoveries due to healthcare interventions and treatment efforts.

Business Impact¶

Recovery analysis helps assess healthcare effectiveness and pandemic management strategies.

WHO Region Analysis¶

This section compares COVID-19 cases across WHO regions to understand the regional impact of the pandemic.

In [18]:
region = df.groupby('WHO Region')[['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered']].max()
region
Out[18]:
Confirmed Deaths Recovered
WHO Region
Africa 452529 7067 274925
Americas 4290259 148011 1846641
Eastern Mediterranean 293606 15912 255144
Europe 816680 45759 602249
South-East Asia 1480073 33408 951166
Western Pacific 82040 4512 64435

Observation¶

The Americas and South-East Asia recorded the highest numbers of confirmed cases.

Insight¶

COVID-19 affected regions differently due to variations in population, healthcare systems, and government policies.

Business Impact¶

Regional comparisons help policymakers allocate healthcare resources more effectively.

In [19]:
region['Confirmed'].plot(kind='bar')
plt.show()

Global COVID-19 Trend Analysis¶

This analysis examines how confirmed COVID-19 cases changed over time.

In [20]:
daily = df.groupby('Date')[['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered']].sum()
daily['Confirmed'].plot()
plt.title('Global Confirmed Cases Trend')
plt.show()

Observation¶

Confirmed cases increased rapidly throughout the observed period.

Insight¶

The trend demonstrates the rapid global spread of COVID-19 during the pandemic.

Business Impact¶

Trend analysis helps governments and healthcare organizations prepare for future outbreaks.

Feature Engineering and Correlation Analysis¶

New metrics such as Death Rate and Recovery Rate are created to better understand the severity and recovery performance of COVID-19.

In [21]:
df['Death_Rate'] = (df['Deaths']/df['Confirmed'])*100
df['Death_Rate'] = df['Death_Rate'].fillna(0)
In [22]:
df['Recovery_Rate'] = (df['Recovered']/df['Confirmed'])*100
df['Recovery_Rate'] = df['Recovery_Rate'].fillna(0)
In [23]:
num_cols = ['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered','Active']
sns.heatmap(df[num_cols].corr(),
            annot=True,
            cmap='coolwarm')
plt.show()

Observation¶

A strong positive correlation exists between Confirmed Cases, Deaths, Recovered Cases, and Active Cases.

Insight¶

Countries with higher confirmed cases generally experienced higher deaths and recoveries.

Business Impact¶

Correlation analysis helps identify relationships between important pandemic indicators.

Pairwise Relationship Analysis¶

A pairplot is used to visualize relationships between numerical variables.

In [24]:
sns.pairplot(df[num_cols])
plt.show()

Observation¶

Most variables show positive relationships with each other.

Insight¶

As confirmed cases increase, deaths, recoveries, and active cases also tend to increase.

Business Impact¶

Understanding relationships between variables supports data-driven decision-making.

Monthly COVID-19 Analysis¶

This analysis examines how confirmed cases, deaths, and recoveries changed month by month.

In [25]:
df['Month'] = df['Date'].dt.month
monthly = df.groupby('Month')[['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered']].sum()
monthly.plot()
plt.show()

Observation¶

Monthly case counts increased significantly over time.

Insight¶

The pandemic continued to expand during the observed period.

Business Impact¶

Monthly analysis helps identify periods of rapid growth and supports future planning.

Top 10 Most Affected Countries¶

This analysis identifies the countries with the highest numbers of confirmed COVID-19 cases.

In [26]:
country_cases.sort_values('Active',ascending=False).head(10)
Out[26]:
Confirmed Deaths Recovered Active
Country/Region
US 4290259 148011 1325804 2816444
Brazil 2442375 87618 1846641 583080
India 1480073 33408 951166 495499
United Kingdom 300111 45759 533 254352
Russia 816680 13334 602249 245382
South Africa 452529 7067 274925 173590
Colombia 257101 8777 131161 117163
Pakistan 274289 5842 241026 108642
Peru 389717 18418 272547 108616
Italy 246286 35112 198593 108257
In [27]:
country_summary = df.groupby('Country/Region')[['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered','Active']].max()

most_affected = country_summary.sort_values(
    by='Confirmed',
    ascending=False
)

print("Top 10 Most Affected Countries")
print(most_affected.head(10))

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=most_affected.head(10).index,
    y=most_affected.head(10)['Confirmed']
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Top 10 Most Affected Countries")
plt.show()

Top 10 Most Affected Countries
                Confirmed  Deaths  Recovered   Active
Country/Region                                       
US                4290259  148011    1325804  2816444
Brazil            2442375   87618    1846641   583080
India             1480073   33408     951166   495499
Russia             816680   13334     602249   245382
South Africa       452529    7067     274925   173590
Mexico             395489   44022     303810    53572
Peru               389717   18418     272547   108616
Chile              347923    9187     319954    82961
United Kingdom     300111   45759        533   254352
Iran               293606   15912     255144    34887

Observation¶

The United States reported the highest number of confirmed cases, followed by Brazil and India.

Insight¶

A small number of countries accounted for a significant proportion of global cases.

Business Impact¶

Understanding affected countries helps prioritize healthcare and policy interventions.

In [28]:
highest_deaths = country_summary.sort_values(
    by='Deaths',
    ascending=False
)

print(highest_deaths.head(10))

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=highest_deaths.head(10).index,
    y=highest_deaths.head(10)['Deaths']
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Top 10 Countries by Death Count")

Active Case Analysis¶

This analysis identifies countries currently facing the highest numbers of active COVID-19 cases.

In [31]:
daily_cases = df.groupby('Date')[
    ['Confirmed','Deaths','Recovered']
].sum()

plt.figure(figsize=(15,6))

plt.plot(
    daily_cases.index,
    daily_cases['Confirmed'],
    label='Confirmed'
)

plt.plot(
    daily_cases.index,
    daily_cases['Deaths'],
    label='Deaths'
)

plt.plot(
    daily_cases.index,
    daily_cases['Recovered'],
    label='Recovered'
)

plt.legend()
plt.title("Global COVID Growth Trend")
plt.show()
In [32]:
top_active = country_summary.sort_values(
    by='Active',
    ascending=False
).head(10)

print(top_active)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=top_active.index,
    y=top_active['Active']
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Top 10 Countries by Active Cases")
plt.show()

                Confirmed  Deaths  Recovered   Active
Country/Region                                       
US                4290259  148011    1325804  2816444
Brazil            2442375   87618    1846641   583080
India             1480073   33408     951166   495499
United Kingdom     300111   45759        533   254352
Russia             816680   13334     602249   245382
South Africa       452529    7067     274925   173590
Colombia           257101    8777     131161   117163
Pakistan           274289    5842     241026   108642
Peru               389717   18418     272547   108616
Italy              246286   35112     198593   108257

Observation¶

The United States, Brazil, and India recorded the highest active case counts.

Insight¶

These countries experienced significant pressure on healthcare systems.

Business Impact¶

Active case monitoring helps estimate healthcare demand and hospital capacity requirements.

Recovery Rate Analysis¶

Recovery Rate measures the percentage of confirmed cases that have recovered.

In [33]:
country_summary['Recovery Rate (%)'] = (
    country_summary['Recovered']
    / country_summary['Confirmed']
) * 100

recovery_rate = country_summary.sort_values(
    by='Recovery Rate (%)',
    ascending=False
)

print(
    recovery_rate[
        ['Confirmed','Recovered','Recovery Rate (%)']
    ].head(10)
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=recovery_rate.head(10).index,
    y=recovery_rate.head(10)['Recovery Rate (%)']
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Top Recovery Rate Countries")
plt.show()

                Confirmed  Recovered  Recovery Rate (%)
Country/Region                                         
Holy See               12         12         100.000000
Dominica               18         18         100.000000
Grenada                23         23         100.000000
Djibouti             5059       4977          98.379126
Iceland              1854       1823          98.327940
Brunei                141        138          97.872340
New Zealand          1557       1514          97.238279
Qatar              109597     106328          97.017254
Malaysia             8904       8601          96.597035
Mauritius             344        332          96.511628

Observation¶

Several countries achieved recovery rates above 95%.

Insight¶

High recovery rates indicate effective healthcare responses and treatment strategies.

Business Impact¶

Recovery rates help evaluate healthcare system performance.

Death Rate Analysis¶

Death Rate measures the percentage of confirmed cases resulting in death.

In [34]:
country_summary['Death Rate (%)'] = (
    country_summary['Deaths']
    / country_summary['Confirmed']
) * 100

death_rate = country_summary.sort_values(
    by='Death Rate (%)',
    ascending=False
)

print(
    death_rate[
        ['Confirmed','Deaths','Death Rate (%)']
    ].head(10)
)

plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
sns.barplot(
    x=death_rate.head(10).index,
    y=death_rate.head(10)['Death Rate (%)']
)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.title("Highest Death Rate Countries")
plt.show()

                Confirmed  Deaths  Death Rate (%)
Country/Region                                   
Yemen                1691     483       28.562980
United Kingdom     300111   45759       15.247358
Belgium             66428    9822       14.785934
France             208665   30096       14.423118
Italy              246286   35112       14.256596
Hungary              4448     596       13.399281
Netherlands         53151    6141       11.553875
Mexico             395489   44022       11.131030
Spain              272421   28752       10.554252
Western Sahara         10       1       10.000000

Observation¶

Yemen, the United Kingdom, Belgium, France, and Italy reported some of the highest death rates.

Insight¶

Differences in healthcare quality, demographics, and public health responses may influence death rates.

Business Impact¶

Death rate analysis helps identify high-risk regions requiring additional support.

Correlation Matrix¶

The correlation matrix helps identify relationships among Confirmed Cases, Deaths, Recoveries, and Active Cases.

In [35]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))

sns.heatmap(
    df[['Confirmed',
        'Deaths',
        'Recovered',
        'Active']].corr(),
    annot=True,
    cmap='coolwarm'
)

plt.title("Correlation Matrix")
plt.show()

Observation¶

Strong positive correlations exist between most COVID-19 indicators.

Insight¶

Confirmed cases are closely associated with deaths and recoveries.

Business Impact¶

Understanding these relationships improves pandemic monitoring and forecasting.

Key Findings¶

  1. The United States recorded the highest number of confirmed COVID-19 cases.
  2. Brazil and India were among the most affected countries.
  3. The Americas were the most impacted WHO region.
  4. Confirmed cases increased rapidly over time.
  5. Active cases were concentrated in a few countries.
  6. Recovery rates exceeded 95% in several countries.
  7. Death rates varied significantly across countries.
  8. Strong positive correlations existed between confirmed cases, deaths, and recoveries.
  9. Healthcare system effectiveness influenced recovery and mortality outcomes.

Conclusion¶

This project analyzed global COVID-19 data using Python, Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn. Through data cleaning, visualization, and exploratory analysis, important trends and patterns were identified. The analysis revealed the most affected countries and regions, highlighted recovery and mortality patterns, and demonstrated the value of data-driven decision-making during public health emergencies.
